AssetEntry.description: A single concrete file the bundle ships.

path.description: Path inside the bundle (relative to bundle/)
path.title: Path

dest_path.description: Destination path on receiving machine, with ~ expansion deferred to ingest time
dest_path.title: Dest Path

sha256.description: Hex digest of asset bytes — verified on ingest
sha256.title: Sha256

mode_bits.description: POSIX mode bits to restore on ingest (executable bit etc.)
mode_bits.title: Mode Bits

notes.description: Optional human-readable note shown in Briefing Preview
notes.title: Notes

kind.description: v1.1 — concrete asset category for cross-harness mapping. One of 'skill' | 'rule' | 'hook' | 'bin' | 'capability'. 'other' is reserved as a parser-side sentinel and is REJECTED at seal time.
kind.title: Kind

behavior_md.description: v1.1 — short behavioral hint (~200 chars) extracted from the asset source at compose time. Helps non-Claude receivers and human readers see what each asset does without opening the file.
behavior_md.title: Behavior Md

AssetEntry.title: AssetEntry

BriefingSection.description: One section of the 'Dear Receiving Claude' briefing.

content_md.description: Rendered Markdown content for this section
content_md.title: Content Md

BriefingSection.title: BriefingSection

Capability.description: A named bundle of intent + behavior, composed of one or more assets.

intent.description: Why-it-exists, in user's words where possible
intent.title: Intent

dependencies.description: Names of OS-level binaries the destination must have (e.g. ripgrep)
dependencies.title: Dependencies

smoke_commands.description: G6 — shell commands the destination runs post-install to verify the capability is actually wired up. Each must exit 0 within 10 seconds (executed under sh -c with destination HOME). Catches partial installs that file-presence smoke would miss (e.g. skills installed but the binary they call is absent, or hook section never reached because of an unrelated parse error).
smoke_commands.title: Smoke Commands

registered_via.description: C (B G12 adjacent) — when set, this capability was composed from a registry YAML rather than discovery. Path + sha lets the receiver verify provenance and tell registry-composed bundles from discovery-composed ones (otherwise they'd be byte-indistinguishable).

Capability.title: Capability

Confirmation.description: One user y/n decision recorded in confirmations.log.
Confirmation.title: Confirmation

RegistrationRef.description:
C (Hunter B G12 adjacent) — provenance of a registry-composed bundle.

When a capability is composed from `~/.claude/capabilities/<name>.yaml`
(rather than from discovery), the registry path + content sha256 are
stamped here. Receivers can verify the bundle's origin and replay the
same registration semantically.

registry_path.description: Path to the registry YAML on the source machine, with ~/ where applicable
registry_path.title: Registry Path

yaml_sha256.description: Hex sha256 of the registry YAML at compose time
yaml_sha256.title: Yaml Sha256

RegistrationRef.title: RegistrationRef

description:
Top-level manifest — authoritative on the source side.

Shipped at bundle root as `manifest.json`. Consumed by the receiving
`agentbridge-ingest` skill alongside BRIEFING.md.

source_machine_home.description: FR-002 (v1.1 mac-compat) — absolute path of `Path.home()` on the source machine at seal time (e.g. '/home/user' on Linux, '/Users/user' on macOS). Separate from any per-asset `home` fields so the receiver can re-stamp paths into ITS own home without losing provenance. Optional for backward compat: v1.0 bundles do not carry this and ingest falls back to the old behavior (same-OS receiver only).
source_machine_home.title: Source Machine Home

confirmations.description: User Y/N decisions captured by Briefing Preview UI (FR-009, SC-007)
confirmations.title: Confirmations

title: ManifestModel
